title: podocarpus totara subsp. waihoensis
intro: Podocarpus totara subsp. waihoensis is a subspecies of Podocarpus totara, commonly known as the Totara, a native evergreen coniferous tree found in New Zealand. The plant has been used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Podocarpus totara subsp. waihoensis are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used in Māori medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
